title: [SLP] Bail out on store-to-load forwarding hazards
author: mbhade-amd
description:
Problem
=======
When SLP vectorizes a chain of stores into a wide vector store inside a loop, nearby loads that read from earlier iterations (loop-carried backward dependences) can suffer Store-to-Load Forwarding (STLF) stalls.  The hardware can forward data from the store buffer only when the load is fully contained within a single store; if the load straddles two pending vector stores, forwarding fails and the load must wait for the stores to drain to L1 (typically a 10-15 cycle penalty per occurrence).

A canonical example is:

    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++)
        a[i] = a[i-1] + 1;

Scalar code: each iteration's load `a[i-1]` forwards cleanly from the previous iteration's 4-byte store.

After SLP with VF=4: stores become 16-byte writes to `a[i:i+3]`, but the load `a[i-1]` is 4 bytes behind the store base.  Distance (4) is not a multiple of the vector store width (16), so the load straddles two in-flight vector stores and STLF fails.  The vectorized loop runs significantly slower than the scalar version.

Solution
========
Add a target-independent STLF hazard check
(`hasStoreLoadForwardingConflict`) that runs as an early bail-out in `vectorizeStoreChain`, before any tree construction or cost modeling.

The check fires only when *all* of the following hold:

  1. The store chain is inside a loop (no STLF concern outside loops).
  2. The loop body is small (<= 128 instructions by default, tunable via -slp-stlf-loop-size-threshold).  In larger loops, OOO execution amortizes the stall, so we don't reject.
  3. There exists a same-base load in the same block.
  4. The load is a *backward* loop-carried reference (negative pointer diff w.r.t. the stores).
  5. The load distance is at least one vector-store width (otherwise it doesn't overlap the widened region).
  6. The distance is *misaligned* to the vector store width (`Distance % VectorStoreBytes != 0`), so the load straddles two vector stores.
  7. The store is still "hot" in the store buffer (`Distance / VectorStoreBytes < 8 * ElementSize` iterations). Threshold matches `LoopAccessAnalysis::couldPreventStoreLoadForward`.

When all conditions are true, we abort vectorization for this chain and emit an `OptimizationRemarkMissed` (id `StoreLoadForwardConflict`) so users can see why the chain was skipped via
`-Rpass-missed=slp-vectorizer`.

Implementation Notes
====================
- Placed *before* `buildTree`/`reorder`/`transformNodes`/ `getTreeCost`, so rejected chains incur no SLP analysis cost.
- Gated by a hidden, default-on flag `-slp-store-load-forward-check` (set to `false` to disable).
- Loop-body-size threshold is exposed as a hidden cl::opt (`-slp-stlf-loop-size-threshold`, default 128) for easy tuning.
- Defensive guards against scalable types and zero-sized stores so the modulo/division operations cannot trigger UB.
- Uses `LoopInfo`, `DataLayout`, `ScalarEvolution` and `getPointersDiff` -- all already available on `SLPVectorizerPass`, so no new analysis dependencies.
- Heuristic is conservative: it only rejects clearly problematic shapes; positive-diff/forward references and large loops are left to the existing cost model.

Known Limitation
================
The current implementation scans only the basic block of the first store for conflicting loads. In multi-block loops where loads have been hoisted/sunk into a different block (e.g., conditional code), this check may miss STLF-conflicting accesses. Extending the scan to all blocks of the loop is left as a follow-up.

Testing
=======
- Verified bail-out path with `-debug-only=SLP` on the canonical `a[i] = a[i-1] + 1` shape.
- Confirmed remark emission with `-Rpass-missed=slp-vectorizer`.
- No measured regressions on benchmarks where the heuristic does not fire (large loops, forward references, non-loop chains).

cc @alexey-bataev @fhahn

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_pointer_diff_unknown(ptr noalias %A, ptr noalias %IdxA, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %idx.gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %IdxA, i64 %i
  %dyn.idx = load i64, ptr %idx.gep, align 8
  %dyn.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %dyn.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %dyn.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_volatile_load_skipped(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 5, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 5
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load volatile i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_no_conflict_different_base(ptr noalias %A, ptr noalias %B, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 5, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %b.idx = sub i64 %i, 5
  %b.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %B, i64 %b.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %b.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_no_conflict_iter_safety_boundary(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 129, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 129
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_conflict_backward_misaligned_i64(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 3, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 3
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load i64, ptr %back.gep, align 8
  %t1 = add nsw i64 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i64 %t, 2
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  store i64 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 8
  store i64 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 8
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_no_conflict_no_loop(ptr noalias %A) {
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 -5
  %t = load i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %A, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_no_conflict_backward_aligned(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 4, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 4
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_large_loop_bypassed(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 5, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 5
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_no_conflict_forward(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %fwd.idx = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 64
  %fwd.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %fwd.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %fwd.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_multiple_conflicting_loads(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 7, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back5.idx = sub i64 %i, 5
  %back5.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back5.idx
  %t5 = load i32, ptr %back5.gep, align 4
  %back7.idx = sub i64 %i, 7
  %back7.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back7.idx
  %t7 = load i32, ptr %back7.gep, align 4
  %sum = add nsw i32 %t5, %t7
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %sum, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %sum, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %sum, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %sum, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_conflict_backward_misaligned(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 5, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 5
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @stlf_no_conflict_within_prev_store(ptr noalias %A, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.body ]
  %back.idx = sub i64 %i, 1
  %back.gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %back.idx
  %t = load i32, ptr %back.gep, align 4
  %t1 = add nsw i32 %t, 1
  %t2 = add nsw i32 %t, 2
  %t3 = add nsw i32 %t, 3
  %t4 = add nsw i32 %t, 4
  %i1 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 1
  %i2 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 2
  %i3 = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 3
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i1
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i2
  %gep3 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %i3
  store i32 %t1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  store i32 %t2, ptr %gep1, align 4
  store i32 %t3, ptr %gep2, align 4
  store i32 %t4, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %i.next = add nuw nsw i64 %i, 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM NEW CONTRIBUTOR COMMENT-->

Hello @mbhade-amd :wave:

Thank you for submitting a Pull Request (PR) to the LLVM Project. Since this is your first PR, here are a few useful links covering our main contribution policies and review practices.

* All contributions to LLVM must follow our [LLVM AI Tool Use Policy](https://llvm.org/docs/AIToolPolicy.html). In particular, if you used AI while working on this PR, remember to add a note to the PR description.
* The [LLVM Code-Review Policy and Practices](https://llvm.org/docs/CodeReview.html) document contains practical information about the PR process, including how patches are reviewed and accepted, and who can review a PR.
* Our [LLVM Developer Policy](https://llvm.org/docs/DeveloperPolicy.html) describes our expectations for code quality, commit summaries and contains notes on our CI system.

Please reply to this message to confirm that you have read these policies, especially the LLVM AI Tool Use Policy, and that any AI tool usage has been noted in the PR description.

---

### Frequently asked questions

**How do I add reviewers?**

This PR will be automatically labeled, and the relevant teams will be notified. For some parts of the project, reviewers may also be added automatically.

You can also add reviewers manually using the **Reviewers** section on this page. If you cannot use that section, it is probably because you do not have write permissions for the repository. In that case, you can request a review by tagging reviewers in a comment using `@` followed by their GitHub username.

**What if there are no comments?**

If you have not received any comments on your PR after a week, you can request a review by pinging the PR with a comment such as “Ping”. The common courtesy ping rate is once a week. Please remember that you are asking for volunteer time from other developers.

**Are any special GitHub settings required to contribute to LLVM?**

We only require contributors to have a public email address associated with their GitHub commits, see this [section](https://llvm.org/docs/DeveloperPolicy.html#email-addresses) of LLVM Developer Policy for details.

---

If you have questions, feel free to leave a comment on this PR, or ask on [LLVM Discord](https://discord.com/invite/xS7Z362) or [LLVM Discourse](https://discourse.llvm.org/).

Thank you,
The LLVM Community
